terms.definition: Systematic study of human walking, observing stance and swing phases to identify biomechanical abnormalities.
terms.exampleSentence: Gait analysis revealed excessive pronation during midstance contributing to her medial knee pain.
terms.contextNotes: May be observational, video, or pressure-plate based.

terms.definition: Evaluation of joint range, muscle function and lower-limb alignment to identify causes of mechanical foot pathology.
terms.exampleSentence: Biomechanical assessment confirmed forefoot varus requiring functional orthotic prescription.
terms.contextNotes: Includes static (non-weight-bearing) and dynamic components.

terms.definition: Examination of arterial supply to the lower limb using palpation, Doppler and pressure indices to detect ischaemia.
terms.exampleSentence: Vascular assessment showed absent pedal pulses and an ABPI of 0.6, indicating moderate arterial disease.
terms.contextNotes: Mandatory before sharp debridement of any ulcer.

terms.definition: Arterial pulse palpated on the dorsum of the foot lateral to the extensor hallucis longus tendon.
terms.exampleSentence: The dorsalis pedis pulse was palpable bilaterally and of normal volume.
terms.contextNotes: Congenitally absent in up to 10% of the population.

terms.definition: Arterial pulse palpated posterior to the medial malleolus along the tibialis posterior tendon.
terms.exampleSentence: The posterior tibial pulse was diminished on the right, prompting Doppler assessment.
terms.contextNotes: Found behind medial malleolus; more reliable than DP in elderly patients.

terms.definition: Ankle-brachial pressure index; ratio of ankle systolic to brachial systolic pressure used to screen for peripheral arterial disease.
terms.exampleSentence: An ABPI of 0.85 confirmed mild peripheral arterial disease and contraindicated compression bandaging.
terms.contextNotes: Normal 0.9-1.3; falsely elevated in diabetic medial calcification.

terms.definition: Sensory screening using a 10g Semmes-Weinstein nylon filament applied to defined plantar sites to detect protective sensation loss.
terms.exampleSentence: The 10g monofilament test was insensate at four of ten plantar sites, confirming loss of protective sensation.
terms.contextNotes: Cornerstone of NICE diabetic foot screening; loss = high risk.

terms.definition: Sensory modality assessed with a 128Hz tuning fork at the hallux to detect large-fibre peripheral neuropathy.
terms.exampleSentence: Vibration sense was absent at the hallux bilaterally, consistent with diabetic peripheral neuropathy.
terms.contextNotes: Tests dorsal column function; complements monofilament screening.

terms.definition: The body's awareness of joint position and movement in space, mediated by mechanoreceptors in muscle, tendon and joint capsule.
terms.exampleSentence: Impaired proprioception increased her risk of falls and unsteady gait on uneven ground.
terms.contextNotes: Reduced in diabetic neuropathy and after ankle sprain.

terms.definition: Bedside test of arterial sufficiency: leg elevation produces pallor, then dependency produces reactive hyperaemia in ischaemia.
terms.exampleSentence: Buerger's test produced pallor at 30 degrees and rubor on dependency, suggesting significant ischaemia.
terms.contextNotes: Buerger's angle <20 degrees indicates severe ischaemia.

terms.definition: Time taken for colour to return to a blanched nail bed or pulp after pressure release; prolonged in poor perfusion.
terms.exampleSentence: Capillary refill time was prolonged at 5 seconds in the left hallux, indicating impaired perfusion.
terms.contextNotes: Normal <2 seconds; less reliable in cold environments.

terms.definition: Grading system for diabetic foot ulcers from grade 0 (intact at-risk skin) to grade 5 (gangrene of whole foot).
terms.exampleSentence: The plantar ulcer was classified as Wagner grade 2 with exposed tendon but no abscess.
terms.contextNotes: Often paired with University of Texas system for depth/infection.

terms.definition: UK NICE framework classifying diabetic feet as low, moderate, high or active risk to guide screening frequency and referral.
terms.exampleSentence: Per NICE diabetic foot risk stratification she was high risk and referred to the foot protection service.
terms.contextNotes: Drives referral pathway; high risk = podiatry within 2-4 weeks.

terms.definition: Structured evaluation combining history, pulse palpation, ABPI and Doppler waveforms to identify limb ischaemia.
terms.exampleSentence: Peripheral arterial disease assessment confirmed intermittent claudication with an ABPI of 0.7.
terms.contextNotes: Determines safety of compression therapy and debridement.

terms.definition: Non-contact infrared imaging of plantar skin temperature; asymmetry >2.2°C predicts pre-ulcerative inflammation.
terms.exampleSentence: Foot temperature thermography showed a 3°C hotspot under the first metatarsal head, prompting offloading.
terms.contextNotes: Emerging technology for early Charcot and re-ulceration detection.

terms.definition: Examination combining monofilament, vibration, pinprick and ankle reflex testing to detect peripheral neuropathy.
terms.exampleSentence: Neurological assessment of the foot revealed loss of protective sensation and absent ankle reflexes.
terms.contextNotes: Combine multiple modalities; no single test is sensitive enough alone.

terms.definition: Use of handheld Doppler to characterise pedal artery flow as triphasic, biphasic or monophasic, indicating arterial health.
terms.exampleSentence: Doppler waveform analysis revealed monophasic flow in the dorsalis pedis, suggesting significant arterial disease.
terms.contextNotes: More sensitive than palpation for early peripheral arterial disease.

terms.definition: Tendon reflex elicited by tapping the Achilles tendon; loss is an early sign of peripheral neuropathy.
terms.exampleSentence: Ankle reflexes were absent bilaterally, consistent with diabetic peripheral neuropathy.
terms.contextNotes: Tested with patient kneeling or supine with hip externally rotated.

terms.definition: Evaluation of shoe fit, depth, toe-box width and wear pattern to identify footwear-related pathology.
terms.exampleSentence: Footwear assessment showed a tight toe-box contributing to her interdigital corns and hallux valgus pain.
terms.contextNotes: Inspect inside of shoe for foreign bodies in neuropathic patients.

terms.definition: Visual examination of plantar, dorsal and interdigital skin plus all 10 nails for lesions, callus, fissures and discolouration.
terms.exampleSentence: Skin and nail inspection identified a heel fissure and onychomycosis of the first toenail.
terms.contextNotes: Always inspect between toes; macerated webspaces harbour fungal infection.

terms.definition: Measurement of joint movement at the ankle, subtalar, midtarsal and first MTP joints to identify restriction or hypermobility.
terms.exampleSentence: Range of motion assessment showed reduced ankle dorsiflexion of 5 degrees with the knee extended.
terms.contextNotes: Reduced ankle dorsiflexion is a major risk factor for forefoot ulceration.

terms.definition: Clinical test differentiating gastrocnemius from gastroc-soleus equinus by comparing ankle dorsiflexion with knee extended versus flexed.
terms.exampleSentence: Silfverskiöld test confirmed isolated gastrocnemius equinus, indicating suitability for proximal release.
terms.contextNotes: Guides surgical decision between gastroc recession and TAL.

terms.definition: Documentation of ulcer size, depth, base tissue, exudate, edge and surrounding skin to guide treatment and monitor healing.
terms.exampleSentence: Wound assessment recorded a 2x3cm sloughy ulcer with moderate exudate and macerated periwound skin.
terms.contextNotes: Use TIME (tissue, infection, moisture, edge) framework for documentation.

terms.definition: Use of in-shoe or platform sensors to quantify plantar pressure distribution and identify high-risk loading areas.
terms.exampleSentence: Pressure mapping revealed peak pressures under the second metatarsal head exceeding 1000 kPa.
terms.contextNotes: Guides offloading orthotic design in high-risk diabetic feet.

terms.definition: Non-invasive measurement of skin oxygen tension used to assess healing potential of foot ulcers and amputation sites.
terms.exampleSentence: Transcutaneous oxygen pressure of 25 mmHg suggested marginal healing potential at the proposed amputation level.
terms.contextNotes: >40 mmHg predicts good healing; <30 mmHg predicts poor healing.
